source_metadata.title_raw: Michael Torres - A-18-154 - August 23, 2018 - Newport Beach

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 23, 2018

Michael Torres
Assistant City Attorney
100 Civic Center Drive
Newport Beach, California 92660

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-18-154

Dear Mr. Torres:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of City of Newport Beach Planning Commissioner Kory Kramer regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that we are only providing advice under those provisions and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTION

Does the Act prohibit Planning Commissioner Kramer from taking part in governmental decisions relating to the Koll Center Project given that the Commissioner has a source-of-income interest in an unnamed individual who owns multiple commercial real properties located within 500 feet of the Project site?

CONCLUSION

Yes. The Act prohibits the Planning Commissioner from taking part in those decisions because they would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on the Commissioner's source-of-income interest in that individual.

FACTS

You are an Assistant City Attorney for the City of Newport Beach and the authorized representative of Planning Commissioner Kramer. In his private capacity, the Commissioner is a

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-154
Page No. 2

consultant in the private sector. A client of the Commissioner, Lyon Living Development Company
("LLDC"), owns commercial real property located at 4901 Birch Street within the City. The
Commissioner earns more than $500 per year from his consulting work for LLDC.

Shopoff Land Fund II, L.P. has submitted a development application to the City for the Koll
Center Project, a large residential development project comprised of three 13-story condominium
buildings and a parking garage on approximately12.5 acres. The Project site is located at 4400 Von
Karman Avenue and currently consists of parking lots and a commercial office development.

According to Section 1.2.1 of the Draft Environmental Impact Report ("EIR") for the
Project, the "project site is an irregularly-shaped property generally bordered by Birch Street to the
northeast, Von Karman Avenue to the west, and existing office uses and associated surface parking
lots and parking structures to the east and south." The Project site is surrounded to the northwest by
an Extended Stay America Hotel and the Duke Hotel, to the northeast and south by office buildings,
and to the southeast by office buildings, fast food restaurants, a superior courthouse, and a mixed-
use project including commercial and residential uses. The Project will bring high residential
density to a predominantly commercial area. The EIR for the Project concluded the Project would
result in significant impacts, even after mitigation measures are imposed, particularly with respect
to noise and air quality.

An unnamed individual owns a 50 percent stake in LLDC, the owner of 4901 Birch Street,
and also owns two other commercial real properties nearby the Project site, located at 4911 Birch
Street and 4650 Von Karman Drive, respectively. 4901 Birch Street and 4911 Birch Street are
located directly across Birch Street from the Project site, and 4650 Von Karman Drive is located
immediately northeast of those two Birch Street properties. All three properties are located within
approximately 500 feet of the Project site.

ANALYSIS

The Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit a public official from making, participating
in making, or using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision that would have
a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or more of the official's financial interests
distinguishable from the decision's effect on the public generally. (Sections 87100 and 87103.) An
official's financial interests that may give rise to a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act
are set forth in Section 87103 and include all the following:

• Any business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment worth $2,000 or
more (subdivision (a)), or in which the official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee,
or holds any position of management (subdivision (d)).

• Any real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or more.
(Subdivision (b).)

• Any source of income aggregating $500 or more in value to the official in the 12 months prior
to when the decision at issue is made. (Subdivision (c).)

File No. A-18-154
Page No. 3

• Any donor of a gift or gifts aggregating $470 or more to the official in the 12 months prior to
when the decision at issue is made. (Subdivision (e); Regulation 18940.2.)

• An interest in the official's personal finances or those of immediate family members.

With respect to the decisions relating to the Koll Center Project, the facts presented indicate
that Planning Commissioner Kramer has a business interest in his consulting business; a source-of-
income interest in his consulting business; a source-of-income interest in LLDC; and an interest in
his personal finances.

Regulation 18700.1(a)(2)(A) provides that in addition to having a source-of-income interest
in a business entity from which an official has received $500 or more within the 12 months prior to
the decision at issue, the official also has a source-of-income interest in any individual who has an
ownership interest of 50 percent or greater in that business entity. The Commissioner has a source-
of-income interest in LLDC, and an unnamed individual has a 50 percent stake in LLDC.
Therefore, the Commissioner has a source-of-income interest in that individual as well.

Foreseeability and Materiality

Regulation 18701 sets forth the Act's foreseeability standards applicable to a decision's
financial effect on an official's interest. An official's interest is explicitly involved in a decision,
and the effect of that decision on that interest is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable, if the
interest is a named party in, or the subject of, the decision. (Regulation 18701(a).) For an interest
that is not explicitly involved in the decision, the effect on the official's interest is reasonably
foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or
theoretical. (Regulation 18701(b).) None of the Commissioner's interests are explicitly involved in
the decisions relating to Koll Center Project.

Source-of-Income: Unnamed Individual with a 50 Percent Stake in LLDC

Regulation 18702.3(a)(2) provides the materiality standard applicable to a decision's effect
on an official's source-of-income interest in an individual is material if the individual will receive a
measurable benefit or loss from the decision (Regulation 18702.5), or if the official knows or has
reason to know that the individual has a business interest or real-property interest that will be
materially affected by the decision at issue under the materiality standards applicable to that interest
(Regulation 18702.1 and 18702.2, respectively).

Therefore, the decisions relating to the Koll Center Project would have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect on the Commissioner's source-of-income interest in the
unnamed individual with a 50 percent stake in LLDC if those decisions would have a material
effect on any real-property interest or business interest that individual or that individual's interest in
his or her personal finances.

Unnamed Individual's Real-Property Interests

The unnamed individual with a 50 percent stake in LLDC is a source of income to the
Commissioner, and LLDC owns the commercial real property located at 4901 Birch Street. That

File No. A-18-154
Page No. 4

individual also owns two additional commercial real properties located at 4911 Birch Street and
4650 Von Karman Avenue, respectively. Assuming that individual has an interest in each of those
properties worth $2,000 or more, he or she would have real-property interest in each of those
properties. (See Section 87103(b).)

Regulation 18702.2 provides the materiality standards applicable to a decision's effect on an
official's real-property interest. All three of the unnamed individual's commercial real properties
are located within approximately 500 feet of the Project site. Of the materiality standards set forth
in Regulation 18702.2, the following are relevant to the unnamed individual's commercial real-
property interests based on the facts presented, and the effect of a decision relating to the Project is
material if it:

(7) Would change the development potential of the parcel of real property; …

(9) Would change the highest and best use of the parcel of real property in which
the official has an interest; …

(10) Would change the character of the parcel of real property by substantially
altering traffic levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property
surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or
air quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the market
value of the real property parcel in which the official has a financial interest; …

(12) Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and consideration
under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision was of such a
nature that its reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the market value of
the official's property.

The Project will bring high residential density to a predominantly commercial area. The
Project site currently consists of parking structures and a commercial office development. The
Project would replace some of the existing structures at the Project site with three 13-story
condominium buildings and a parking garage. An influx of a substantial number of residents would
increase the intensity of use, traffic levels, and parking demand in the area surrounding the Project.
Additionally, the EIR concluded that the Project may cause significant noise and air quality impacts
in the surrounding area even after the implementation of mitigation measures.

Furthermore, permitting residential development in this predominantly commercial area
may enable future residential development in the surrounding area, including development of the
unnamed individual's commercial real properties within 500 feet of the Project.

Therefore, the decisions relating to the Project would have a reasonably foreseeable material
financial effect on the unnamed individual's real-property interests in 4901 Birch Street, 4910 Birch
Street, and 6250 Von Karman Avenue and, by extension, the Commissioner's source-of-income

File No. A-18-154
Page No. 5

interest in that individual. Thus, the Act prohibits the Commissioner from taking part in decisions relating to the Koll Center Project.²

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Assistant General Counsel

By: Matthew F. Christy
Counsel, Legal Division

MFC:jgl

_______________

² We do not further analyze the Commissioner or the unnamed individual's other financial interests because we have already determined that the Act would prohibit the Commissioner from taking part in decisions relating to the Project because those decisions would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on his source-of-income interest in that unnamed individual.

sections.question:
Does the Act prohibit Planning Commissioner Kramer from taking part in governmental 
decisions relating to the Koll Center Project given that the Commissioner has a source-of-income 
interest in an unnamed individual who owns multiple commercial real properties located within 500 
feet of the Project site?
sections.conclusion:
Yes. The Act prohibits the Planning Commissioner from taking part in those decisions 
because they would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on the Commissioner's . 
source-of-income interest in that individual.

sections.analysis:
The Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit a public official from making, participating 
in making, or using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision that would have 
a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or more of the official's financial interests 
distinguishable from the decision's effect on the public generally. (Sections 87100 and 87103.) An 
official's financial interests that may give rise to a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act 
are set forth in Section 87103 and include all the following: 
• 
Any business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment worth $2,000 or 
more (subdivision (a)), or in which the official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, 
or holds any position of management (subdivision (d)). 
• 
Any real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or more. 
(Subdivision (b ). ) 
• 
Any source of income aggregating $500 or more in value to the official in the 12 months prior 
to when the decision at issue is made. (Subdivision (c).)  
• 
Any donor of a gift or gifts aggregating $4 70 or more to the official in the 12 months prior to 
when the decision at issue is made. (Subdivision (e); Regulation 18940.2.) 
• 
An interest in the official's personal finances or those of immediate family members. 
With respect to the decisions relating to the Koll Center Project, the facts presented indicate 
that Planning Commissioner Kramer has a business interest in his consulting business; a source-of-
income interest in his consulting business; a source-of-income interest in LLDC; and an interest in 
his personal finances. 
Regulation 18700.1 (a)(2)(A) provides that in addition to having a source-of-income interest 
in a business entity from which an official has received $500 or more within the 12 months prior to 
the decision at issue, the official also has a source-of-income interest in any individual who has an 
ownership interest of 50 .percent or greater in that business entity. The Commissioner has a source-
of-income interest in LLDC, and an unnamed individual has a 50 percent stake in LLDC. 
Therefore, the Commissioner has a source-of-income interest in that individual as well. 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
Regulation 18701 sets forth the Act's foreseeability standards applicable to a decision's 
financial effect on an official's interest. An official's interest is explicitly involved in a decision, 
and the effect of that decision on that interest is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable, if the 
interest is a named party in, or the subject of, the decision. (Regulation 18701(a).) For an interest 
that is not explicitly involved in the decision, the effect on the official's interest is reasonably 
foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or 
theoretical. (Regulation 18701(b).) None of the Commissioner's interests are explicitly involved in 
the decisions relating to Koll Center Project. 
Source-of-Income: Unnamed Individual with a 50 Percent Stake in LLDC 
Regulation 18702.3( a)(2) provides the materiality standard applicable to a decision's effect 
on an official's source-of-income interest in an individual is material if the individual will receive a 
measurable benefit or loss from the decision (Regulation 18702.5), or if the official knows or has 
reason to know that the individual has a business interest or real-property interest that will be 
materially affected by the decision at issue under the materiality standards applicable to that interest 
(Regulation 18702.1 and 18702.2, respectively). 
Therefore, the decisions relating to the Koll Center Project would have a reasonably 
foreseeable material financial effect on the Commissioner's source-of-income interest in the 
unnamed individu.al with a 50 percent stake in LLDC if those decisions would have a material 
effect on any real-property interest or business interest that ingividual or that individual's interest in 
his or her personal finances. 
Unnamed Individual's Real-Property Interests 
The unnamed individual with a 50 percent stake in LLDC is a source of income to the 
Commissioner, and LLDC owns the commercial real property located at 4901 Birch Street. That  
individual also owns two additional commercial real properties located at 4911 Birch Street and 
4650 Von Karman Avenue, respectively. Assuming that individual has an interest in each of those 
properties worth $2,000 or more, he or she would have real-property interest in each of those 
properties. (See Section 87103(b).) 
Regulation 18702.2 provides the materiality standards applicable to a decision's effect on an 
official's real-property interest. All three of the unnamed individual's commercial real properties 
are located within approximately 500 feet of the Project site. Of the materiality standards set forth 
in Regulation 18702.2, the following are relevant to the unnamed individual's commercial real-
property interests based on the facts presented, and the effect of a decision relating to the Project is 
material if it: 
(7) Would change the development potential of the parcel of real property; ... 
(9) Would change the highest and best use of the parcel of real property in which 
the official has an interest; ... 
( 10) Would change the character of the parcel of real property by substantially 
altering traffic levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property 
surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or 
air quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the market 
value of the real property parcel in which the official has a financial interest; ... 
(12) Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and consideration 
under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision was of such a 
nature that its reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the market value of 
the official's property. 
The Project will bring high residential density to a predominantly commercial area. The 
Project site currently consists of parking structures and a commercial office development. The 
Project would replace some of the existing structures at the Project site with three 13-story 
condominium buildings and a parking garage. An influx of a substantial number of residents would 
increase the intensity of use, traffic levels, and parking demand in the area surrounding the Project. 
Additionally, the EIR concluded that the Project may cause significant noise and air quality impacts 
in the surrounding area even after the implementation of mitigation measures. 
Furthermore, permitting residential development in this predominantly commercial area 
may enable future residential development in the surrounding area, including development of the 
unnamed individual's commercial real properties within 500 feet of the Project. 
Therefore, the decisions relating to the Project would have a reasonably foreseeable material 
financial effect on the unnamed individual's real-property interests in 4901 Birch Street, 4910 Birch 
Street, and 6250 Von Karman Avenue and, by extension, the Commissioner's source-of-income  
interest in that individual. Thus, the Act prohibits the Commissioner from taking part in decisions 
relating to the Koll Center Project. 2

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Act prohibit Planning Commissioner Kramer from taking part in governmental 
decisions relating to the Koll Center Project given that the Commissioner has a source-of-income 
interest in an unnamed individual who owns multiple commercial real properties located within 500 
feet of the Project site?

CONCLUSION: Yes. The Act prohibits the Planning Commissioner from taking part in those decisions 
because they would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on the Commissioner's . 
source-of-income interest in that individual.
